lessonSections.title: Welcome and Evidence Portfolio Roadmap
lessonSections.content: Welcome to C08-M04 - Transversal Competence Mapping. This lesson explains the portfolio evidence you will produce and how the module links to the FCEI improvement cycle.

activity.prompt: Review the module outcome and tick that you understand what evidence you need to produce.

lessonSections.title: Turning Values into Daily Practice
lessonSections.content: Transversal Competence Mapping is visible when the principle can be seen in learner experience, educator or leader behaviour, and an artefact that proves what changed.

activity.prompt: Write one observable behaviour that would show this principle in your classroom, team or institution.
Plan one support route, one core route and one extension route for the same learning aim.

lessonSections.title: Synthesizing Evidence and Embedding Improvements
lessonSections.content: Upload phenomenon project canvas + subject outcome map + transversal competence evidence. Add date, context, anonymisation note where learner data is used, and a 100-word explanation of what the evidence shows.

activity.prompt: Explain what your artefact shows and what should improve next.

lessonSections.title: Final Performance and Evidence Check
lessonSections.content: Complete the three-question knowledge check and final performance task. The module is not complete until evidence, reflection and the Transferability Filter are submitted.

quiz.question: What is the most responsible first move when applying Transversal Competence Mapping in a new local context?
options: Copy the Finnish practice exactly without change.
Test one manageable action, collect evidence and adapt before scaling.
Wait until every condition is perfect before trying anything.
quiz.rationale: The best answer protects learners and staff through a bounded evidence-first trial.

quiz.question: Which evidence best proves that Transversal Competence Mapping changed practice?
options: The required artefact is completed and linked to a reflection: phenomenon project canvas + subject outcome map + transversal competence evidence.
A general statement that participants enjoyed the topic.
A certificate issued before evidence is uploaded.
quiz.rationale: FCEI completion must show implementation, evidence and reflection, not participation only.

quiz.question: What should be avoided when implementing Transversal Competence Mapping?
options: Running decorative projects that are weakly connected to subject outcomes and evidence.
Keeping the same learning aim while adapting access or process.
Writing the change into a local plan or routine.
quiz.rationale: This distractor is the exact misconception the module is designed to correct.

activity.performanceTask: In your own context, plan one mini-phenomenon task with a real question, two subject links and one evidence checkpoint. Keep the action small enough to complete, review and improve without requiring whole-school rollout first.

lessonSections.title: Wrap Up and Next Steps
lessonSections.content: Review your completion checklist and prepare to continue to the next module.
